Title: Configurable Product PDP Renders Configuration Widget Correctly
Preconditions: Storefront accessible at ${FRONT_URL}. Product Bike with options (SKU: CVQ-54616437) exists at /products-with-options/configurations/build-the-bike-of-your-dreams/bike-with-options
Steps:
1. Navigate to ${FRONT_URL}/products-with-options/configurations/build-the-bike-of-your-dreams/bike-with-options
2. Wait for page to fully render (wait for "Configure" text to appear)
3. Observe the product name at top of page
4. Observe the base product price
5. Observe the Configure the parameters widget
6. Observe the accordion section label Select one
7. Observe the subtitle Personalize your selection further (optional)
8. Observe the accordion is open (expanded) by default
9. Verify 5 radio options are visible: Rear wheel 26 double-wall rim motorized; 200CC 250CC 4-Stroke Engine Motor; Seat; Pedals; None
10. Verify None is selected by default
11. Verify quantity stepper shows 0
12. Verify Add to cart button is disabled when qty = 0
Expected Result:
- Product name Bike with options visible
- Base price $350.00 displayed
- Configure the parameters widget header visible
- Accordion label reads Select one with subtitle Personalize your selection further (optional)
- All 5 radio options present with None selected by default
- Quantity stepper starts at 0
- Add to cart button disabled at qty 0
- In stock badge visible

Title: Selecting Radio Option Updates Total Price Immediately
Preconditions: Storefront accessible. Bike with options PDP loaded. None selected by default. Base price $350.00 visible.
Steps:
1. Note base price: $350.00 (None selected)
2. Click radio button for Seat option
3. Observe total price update
4. Click radio button for Pedals option
5. Observe total price update
6. Click radio button for 200CC 250CC 4-Stroke Engine Motor option
7. Observe total price update
8. Click radio button for Rear wheel 26 double-wall rim motorized option
9. Observe total price update
10. Click None radio button
11. Observe total price returns to base
Expected Result:
- Selecting Seat: total = $365.00 ($350 + $15 x 1)
- Selecting Pedals: total = $364.00 ($350 + $14 x 1)
- Selecting Engine: total = $575.00 ($350 + $225 x 1)
- Selecting Rear wheel: total = $526.00 ($350 + $88 x 2 = $350 + $176)
- Selecting None: total returns to $350.00
- Only one radio selectable at a time
- Price updates are immediate (no page reload required)
- Accordion subtitle updates to show selected option name

Title: Each Option Row Displays Unit Price Quantity and Extended Price
Preconditions: Bike with options PDP loaded. Accordion open showing all option rows.
Steps:
1. Navigate to Bike with options PDP and wait for Configure text
2. Observe Rear wheel 26 double-wall rim motorized row: read unit price, quantity, and extended price
3. Observe 200CC 250CC 4-Stroke Engine Motor row: read unit price, quantity, and extended price
4. Observe Seat row: read unit price, quantity, and extended price
5. Observe Pedals row: read unit price, quantity, and extended price
6. Observe None row: verify no price shown
7. Verify extended price = unit price x quantity for each option
Expected Result:
- Rear wheel: unit $88.00 (salePrice) with $126.00 strikethrough (listPrice), qty 2, extended $176.00
- Engine: unit $225.00, qty 1, extended $225.00
- Seat: unit $15.00, qty 1, extended $15.00
- Pedals: unit $14.00, qty 1, extended $14.00
- None: no price shown
- Extended price = unit price x quantity for all options with prices

Title: Sale Price Shown as Primary Price with List Price Strikethrough
Preconditions: Bike with options PDP loaded. Rear wheel option has salePrice $88.00 and listPrice $126.00.
Steps:
1. Navigate to Bike with options PDP
2. Locate Rear wheel 26 double-wall rim motorized option row
3. Observe the price display on that option row
4. Click the Rear wheel radio button
5. Observe the total product price update
Expected Result:
- Rear wheel option shows $88.00 as the primary/sale price
- $126.00 appears with strikethrough formatting as the list price
- Selecting Rear wheel: total updates to $526.00 (base $350 + extended $176 using salePrice)
- List price $126.00 is NOT used in price calculations
- Visual hierarchy: sale price prominent, list price secondary

Title: Quantity Stepper Starts at Zero and Controls Add to Cart Enable State
Preconditions: Bike with options PDP loaded. Page in fresh state.
Steps:
1. Observe quantity stepper initial value
2. Observe Add to cart button state at qty 0
3. Attempt to click Add to cart while qty = 0
4. Click the + button to increment quantity to 1
5. Observe Add to cart button state
6. Click + again to increase to 2
7. Click - to decrease to 1
8. Click - again to decrease to 0
9. Observe Add to cart button state
10. Attempt to enter negative quantity in field if editable
Expected Result:
- Quantity stepper initializes at 0 on page load
- Add to cart disabled (greyed out or not clickable) at qty 0
- Clicking Add to cart at qty 0 has no effect
- At qty 1: Add to cart button becomes enabled
- At qty 2: button remains enabled
- Decrement to 1: button remains enabled
- Decrement to 0: Add to cart disabled again
- Negative quantities not accepted

Title: No Auto-Scroll on Radio Button Click (VCST-4612 Regression)
Preconditions: Bike with options PDP loaded. Scroll position at the Configure the parameters widget. None selected.
Steps:
1. Scroll to configure widget; note current scroll position (scrollY)
2. Click radio for 200CC 250CC 4-Stroke Engine Motor (very long name causing 2-line subtitle)
3. Verify scroll position unchanged (no page jump)
4. Click radio for Seat (short name, 1-line subtitle)
5. Verify scroll position unchanged
6. Click radio for Pedals
7. Verify scroll position unchanged
8. Alternate between Engine and Seat several times
9. Verify no scroll occurs on any radio click
Expected Result:
- Clicking any radio button does NOT change page scroll position
- scrollY remains constant across all radio button transitions
- No auto-scroll on subtitle line count change (2 lines to 1 line or vice versa)
- Regression check for VCST-4612 fix in Sprint 26-03

Title: Price Calculation Accuracy for All Five Option Combinations
Preconditions: Bike with options PDP loaded. Base price $350.00. Option prices: Rear wheel $88.00 x2=$176; Engine $225.00 x1; Seat $15.00 x1; Pedals $14.00 x1.
Steps:
1. Record base price with None selected: $350.00
2. Select Seat; verify total = $365.00 ($350 + $15)
3. Select Pedals; verify total = $364.00 ($350 + $14)
4. Select Engine; verify total = $575.00 ($350 + $225)
5. Select Rear wheel; verify total = $526.00 ($350 + $88 x 2)
6. Select None; verify total = $350.00
7. Repeat the full cycle once more to check for stale state
Expected Result:
- None: $350.00
- Seat: $365.00 ($350 + $15 x 1)
- Pedals: $364.00 ($350 + $14 x 1)
- Engine: $575.00 ($350 + $225 x 1)
- Rear wheel: $526.00 ($350 + $88 x 2)
- None again: $350.00
- All prices correct on second cycle (no stale state)

Title: E2E: Create Bike Product with Single Optional Radio Section in Admin and Verify on Storefront
Preconditions: Admin logged in at ${BACK_URL} with ${ADMIN} / ${ADMIN_PASSWORD}. Catalog module accessible. Storefront accessible at ${FRONT_URL}.
Steps:
ADMIN STEPS:
1. Navigate to ${BACK_URL} and log in
2. Go to Catalog > Products > Add new product
3. Name: Test Config Bike [YYYYMMDD]; SKU: BIKE-CFG-[YYYYMMDD]
4. Set base List Price: $200.00. Set inventory: 20
5. Navigate to configurable/configuration tab
6. Add section: name=Choose Upgrade; type=Radio; set as Optional
7. Add option: None; $0; qty 20
8. Add option: Basic Seat; $15; qty 10
9. Add option: Premium Seat; $45; qty 5
10. Add option: Racing Seat; $95; qty 2
11. Publish and save product
FRONTEND STEPS:
12. Navigate to ${FRONT_URL} and open PDP for Test Config Bike [YYYYMMDD]
13. Verify Configure the parameters widget is visible
14. Verify section Choose Upgrade shows all 4 options as radio buttons
15. Verify None is selected by default; price = $200.00
16. Select Basic Seat; verify price = $215.00 ($200 + $15)
17. Select Premium Seat; verify price = $245.00 ($200 + $45)
18. Select Racing Seat; verify price = $295.00 ($200 + $95)
19. Return to None; verify price = $200.00
Expected Result:
- Product created in Admin with 4 options under one optional radio section
- Storefront shows Configure the parameters widget with Choose Upgrade section
- All 4 options present and selectable as radio buttons
- Price updates correctly: None=$200 / Basic Seat=$215 / Premium Seat=$245 / Racing Seat=$295
- Default selection is None at $200.00

Title: E2E: Add Configured Product with Quantity Exceeding Option Stock — Backend Enforces Stock Limit
Section: Configurable Products > E2E Quantity Edge Cases
Preconditions: CFG-E2E-001 completed. Product Test Config Bike [YYYYMMDD] published at ${FRONT_URL}. Racing Seat option has inventory quantity=2. NOTE: this is intentional low-stock for this edge-case test.
Steps:
ADMIN STEPS (verify stock):
1. Navigate to ${BACK_URL} and log in as ${ADMIN}
2. Open Test Config Bike [YYYYMMDD] in Catalog > Products; navigate to configurable/configuration tab
3. Verify Racing Seat option shows inventory quantity=2; if changed by a previous test run; reset it to 2 before proceeding
FRONTEND STEPS:
4. Navigate to PDP for Test Config Bike [YYYYMMDD] on ${FRONT_URL}
5. Select Racing Seat in Choose Upgrade; verify price = $295.00
6. Locate the quantity input field on the PDP; set quantity to 5 (exceeds available stock of 2 for Racing Seat)
7. Click Add to Cart
8. Verify one of the following expected behaviors: (a) add-to-cart blocked with stock error message (e.g. Only 2 available for Racing Seat); OR (b) quantity automatically capped at 2 and item added with quantity=2 with informational message
9. Verify system does NOT silently add item with quantity=5 when only 2 are in stock
10. Navigate to cart; verify no line item exists for quantity=5 Racing Seat
11. If quantity was capped at 2: verify line item shows quantity=2 at $590.00 (2 x $295.00)
12. Attempt to manually update cart quantity from 2 to 5 directly in the cart; verify same stock enforcement applies
Expected Result:
- Ordering quantity 5 of Racing Seat (stock=2) is either blocked with stock error or capped at 2
- System never silently accepts quantity=5 when only 2 units are available
- Cart quantity never exceeds available stock for the selected option
- Stock enforcement applies both on PDP add-to-cart and cart quantity update
